stories.text: On May 26, a healthcare executive received a letter from the FDA that created an existential legal crisis for his company — and his career. Days later, he sent $1 million to MAGA Inc. (1/2)

stories.text: Q: You've been blunt in your anti-communist language about China. But do you think it's inconsistent you haven't addressed same issues w/ China? It's the largest communist party in the world RUBIO: Yeah, but our issue is with communist interference inside our country (So much for Trump's speech!)

stories.author: Drop Site team
stories.title: Israel‘s Secret Sales Pitch to Provide Surveillance Drones to the UAE
stories.text: This is essential to our mission: to report on what matters most, beholden only to the truth. In that spirit, we made a commitment to ensure that our journalism is free for everyone, not locked behind a paywall. But that means we rely on the voluntary support of our community of readers. On May 12, U.S. Ambassador to Israel Mike Huckabee publicly disclosed a significant development in the military relationship between Israel and the United Arab Emirates amid the war in Iran. “Israel just sent [the UAE] Iron Dome batteries and personnel to help operate them,” Huckabee said at an event in Tel Aviv. Huckabee described the deployment as a fruit of the Abraham Accords, calling the Israeli-Emirati relationship “extraordinary.” The deployment—as Iranian missiles and drones targeted the region in response to a joint U.S.-Israeli attack—marked the first publicly acknowledged presence of Israeli military personnel and air-defense equipment inside a Gulf monarchy. But the integration of Israeli weapons into the UAE’s defenses did not begin with the Iran war. An archive drawn from the email account of Vered Haimovich, a former Israeli military officer who became vice president of Elbit Systems

stories.author: The Lever team
stories.title: The AI Boom Runs On Toxic Air
stories.text: Drive through the heart of the Permian Basin in West Texas, and the scale of the area’s oil and gas industry is undeniable. Tens of thousands of active wells, drill rigs, pipeline corridors, and flare stacks cover this 86,000-square-mile stretch of West Texas and southeastern New Mexico. At night, flare after flare burns orange against the dark sky. The Permian is the most productive oil field in the United States, responsible for roughly 40 percent of total U.S. oil and gas production. It is also, per some of the most recent global accounting, the single largest source of greenhouse gas-emitting pollution on the planet. In 2022, Climate TRACE, a nonprofit that tracks greenhouse gas emissions via satellite, estimated the basin released over 200 million metric tons of greenhouse gases , more than any other individual facility, field, or infrastructure site on Earth. It is here that the world’s largest technology companies are racing to build massive artificial intelligence data center campuses, powered by yet more natural gas. Texas has more than 80 gigawatts of new power plants planned, roughly half of them for data centers, which will lock in decades of greenhouse gas emissions an

stories.author: Judd Legum
stories.title: Amid legal crisis, health care exec sends $1 million to MAGA Inc.
stories.text: On May 26, the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) sent a letter to QOL Medical, a pharmaceutical company, with some serious allegations. The potential consequences pose an existential threat to the company. In response, QOL Medical’s CEO, Frederick “Derick” Cooper, wrote a $1 million check to MAGA Inc., President Trump’s super PAC. QOL Medical sells Sucraid, a drug that treats congenital sucrase-isomaltase deficiency (CSID), a rare but serious medical condition. Sucraid is effective at treating CSID but is very expensive. The list price can exceed $12,000 per month or $150,000 per year. The FDA letter accused QOL Medical of using “false or misleading” advertising to sell more Sucraid. The marketing materials, according to the FDA, encouraged doctors to prescribe Sucraid to “patients suffering with unresolved IBS-like symptoms, including gas, bloating, diarrhea, and/or nausea.” But Sucraid cannot treat irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) or its symptoms. It can only be used to treat “sucrase deficiency.” Overall, the FDA said, the advertising creates a “misleading impression.” As a result, the FDA informed QOL Medical that it was in violation of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act ,

stories.author: The Lever team
stories.title: Francesca Hong Wants To Be America’s First Socialist Governor
stories.text: LISTEN NOW: The Wisconsin Democratic primary for governor has gone haywire. Over the last week, Lt. Gov. Sara Rodriguez dropped out amid a campaign finance scandal, and a candidate who had suspended his campaign, Milwaukee County Executive David Crowley, rejoined the race — immediately nabbing an influential endorsement from current Gov. Tony Evers. Meanwhile, state Rep. Francesca Hong, a democratic socialist, has surged in recent polls. Is this last-minute shakeup a coincidence, or part of a concerted effort to keep Hong from securing the nomination? Today on Lever Time , David Sirota speaks with Hong, the candidate in the middle of it all. They discuss the opposition to her grassroots campaign, why she thinks her politics are better positioned to defeat Republicans, and why she believes the entire debate over “electability” misunderstands this political moment in the United States. Click here to access your ad-free premium feed . Want essential 2026 midterm coverage? Check out The Lever ’s new weekly newsletter, Midterm Madness , for election updates. More podcast episodes from The Lever : We’re Still Living In The 2016 Election The Case Conservatives Never Forgave Abdul El-Sayed

stories.author: Drop Site team
stories.title: The Gulf War and the Battle For Michigan
stories.text: As the U.S. continues to carry out an air campaign against Iran and attempts to wrest back control of the Strait of Hormuz, the war is already reshaping the future of Gulf Arab states that rely on the critical waterway, as well as the protection of U.S. military bases in the region. On this week’s stream, Ryan Grim and Murtaza Hussain discuss the impact of the war on the GCC states with Amir Handjani, an analyst on the region and board member at the Quincy Institute. In addition, Grim and Hussain discuss the upcoming Democratic primary races in Michigan with progressive 7th Congressional District candidate Will Lawrence, as well as Michigan political reporter Sam Robinson.

stories.author: Drop Site team
stories.title: In Southern Lebanon, a Gloss of Diplomacy Can't Hide the Reality of Occupation
stories.text: Much of Al-Mansouri lies in ruins, with the Israeli-occupied village of Majdal Zoun just across the valley. Despite the destruction and continued Israeli attacks, around 100 residents have chosen to remain. Al Mansouri, Lebanon. AL-MANSOURI, Lebanon—The road into Al-Mansouri winds through a shattered landscape. A barber shop stands without walls, its broken mirror still clinging to the remaining structure. The village cemetery is scarred by craters. The mosque is still standing, though parts of its façade were ripped away by Israeli airstrikes. Nevertheless, over the past month, signs of life have returned to Al-Mansouri, a village in the Sour (Tyre) district near the coastline in southern Lebanon. On Thursday, a handful of men sat on plastic chairs outside their homes, sipping small cups of coffee. Others swept dust from damaged shops. Men on scooters wove between the ruins. The same cannot be said for the neighboring village of Majdal Zoun which lies perched on a hill overlooking Al-Mansouri just a few hundred meters away. The two villages are closely intertwined and even share areas of territory that overlap. Last month, Israeli ground troops invaded and occupied Majdal Zoun, wh

stories.author: The Lever team
stories.title: Gas Grab
stories.text: This is a web version of The Lever ’s daily email newsletter. 🔥 ICYMI in The Lever : Freshly off the hook, the Winklevoss twins are returning the president’s favor . 👇 Further down in this newsletter: Whose pockets your gas money is lining. The data center showdown in the desert. A billion-dollar victory for human creators. STORIES TO GO In a hurry? These are the top stories in the country today. 🛑 A judge has paused the Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery merger, pending state antitrust litigation. 🏛️ A federal appeals court ruled that New Jersey’s ban on assault weapons is unconstitutional , the first time such a court has struck down a state ban on semi-automatic guns. 🎩 Labour Party leader Andy Burnham promised to “ bring back hope ” as he became Great Britain’s seventh prime minister in the past 10 years. SPONSORED If you want to understand the roots of America's unfinished battles over race, democracy, and political power, They Stole A City is essential reading . In a groundbreaking nonfiction history, New Yorker Staff Writer Lauren Collins delivers a full accounting of the long-term effects of a shocking act of racial terrorism and the only successful coup d’état on U.S. soil.

stories.author: Drop Site team
stories.title: Attacks in the Strait of Hormuz; Israel kills 57 Palestinians in Gaza in one week; No more elections in Nicaragua, Ortega says
stories.text: U.S. strikes Iran for tenth straight night. Iranian issues waves of retaliatory attacks. Nearly 100 U.S. service members wounded this month, CBS reports. Iran’s IRGC says it will repel any U.S. incursion into Iran. Multiple attacks in the Strait of Hormuz. Shipowners offering bonuses to crews transiting Hormuz. Iran confirms Turkey helped block Kurdish insurgency plan. Israeli Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich says Israel has “no interest” in joining Iran war. Israeli attacks continue in Lebanon despite U.S.-backed “ceasefire.” “Pilot zone” operations begin in Lebanon; Lebanese Army reports being fired on by Israel. Israel continues escalated assault on Gaza. Israeli attacks in the occupied West Bank. France urges European Union to ban imports from illegal Israeli settlements. Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas reportedly planning election law change that could block Marwan Barghouti from Palestinian Liberation Organization leadership. Man killed fleeing ICE in Florida was on a valid tourist visa. Department of Homeland Security revives rule tying immigrant benefit use to green card eligibility. Judge temporarily blocks Paramount’s $111 billion Warner Bros. Discovery deal. Departm

stories.author: Bellingcat
stories.title: Bellingcat has been monitoring student protests in India. Police initially denied reports protesters were met ...
stories.text: Bellingcat has been monitoring student protests in India. Police initially denied reports protesters were met with violence at a July 20 march, although later acknowledged injuries to protesters and officers. Social media videos showed tear gas deployed and batons used by police against protestors.
